segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They slipped out at first light, when Millbrook's smoke was still thin and blue and the streets were quiet except for scavenging crows.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The palisade gate stood propped with a beam Thornik had reset the night before -- his last job in the town -- and it creaked in the morning chill, wood resin sharp in the air.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The farewells were already done. They'd happened at a mile-stone a mile back, where the road forked three ways and people they trusted had taken two of the other turnings. What remained was just four shadows crossing frost-stiff grass, heading northeast.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin set an easy pace. The Ember's coal-glow tucked beneath a fresh wrap. The ribbon Nyxara had knotted at his wrist lay soft and cool against skin, its silver thread no darker than yesterday. He didn't tug at it. He resisted the urge to.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Your stride's heavier,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex said eventually, mismatched eyes flicking to his arm and away again. She was moving carefully herself -- her ribs logged every twist of the torso, and she'd learned to stop twisting.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Hurts more in the cold,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin admitted.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "It'll level out."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Durgan walked rear guard, winter-pale and deliberate. He scanned the hedgerows and the ditch lines, not the road. When he stepped, his shadow stepped with him -- with an effort most people wouldn't have noticed, but Vex did. She pretended not to.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Aldric walked between Durgan and the rest, which seemed to be where he'd decided he fit. He carried his medical bag slung across one shoulder and moved with the unhurried economy of a man who'd learned to conserve what he had a long time ago.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He hadn't said anything since the mile-stone bend. He didn't appear to need to.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: By the time the road bent past the last shepherd's croft, the smells had changed from dung smoke and malt to iron-rich streamwater and thawed loam. A farmer had nailed a square of parchment to a boundary post. Wind worried its corners.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex plucked it free, skimmed, snorted.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "You're famous."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin didn't need the illustration to know. The crude sketch got the angles of his face wrong but captured the impossible: a black mark in a man's forearm.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Alive,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex read.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "One thousand gold. More for the mark intact."

segments.speaker: durgan
segments.text: "Price is climbing,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Durgan said, voice flat.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Means we're interesting,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex replied.

segments.speaker: durgan
segments.text: "Hate that."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Aldric leaned over and read the poster without taking it from Vex.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "The illustration's generous. You've got better cheekbones than that."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He walked on.

segments.speaker: durgan
segments.text: "Also a thousand gold is insultingly low for whatever that thing in your arm actually is. Someone's being stingy."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They left the sign flapping on its nail. Crows watched from a hedge, heads cocked.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Two miles on, at a wayside shrine toppled by frost heave, Caelin dropped to a knee to tighten his boot. When he cinched the lace and reached for his pack, his fingers brushed paper that hadn't been there when they left town.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He froze. Then palmed it out.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Cream stock. No seal. Three words in an elegant hand that shifted colors in the light like oil on water:

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: North by iron. Two days' grace. -- L

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: A prismatic dusting ran across the letters and vanished.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex caught the flick of paper.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Problem?"

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Map note,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin said, folding it into his sleeve. Not a lie. Not the truth.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: She eyed him a beat longer than was comfortable, then nodded once.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Good. We could use a map."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They rose and walked. The road narrowed to a wheel-track pressed between thorn and alder. Somewhere upslope, a hawk cried. The sky was pale, brittle, clean.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They cut off the road at noon to skirt a flooded bend and followed a sheep path along a hedgerow prickled with last year's hawthorn. Water ran high in the ditch -- brown and cold, tea-bitter with tannin. The air smelled of wet wool, iron, and green things just remembering how to live.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex stopped so abruptly Caelin nearly walked into her. One gloved hand angled down: halt.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: She'd seen it: a drag across the ditch slime where something heavy had been pulled, reeds crushed in a neat human pattern. On the hedge's far side, the faintest glint of brass where no brass should be.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Trip-line,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: she breathed.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Caltrops behind it."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: From the hedge ahead, a voice with too much bravado and not enough patience:

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Easy now. Hands up, no tricks."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Three men broke the hedge at once -- two with crossbows, one with a short fowling spear. They wore leather jacks with mismatched studs and the expressions of people who hadn't been paid yet.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Don't,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex said to Caelin without looking.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "You'll break yourself on farmers."

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Bounty's for alive,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: the spear carrier said, eyes on Caelin.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "We're not monsters."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Behind Caelin, something thinned in the light. Vex didn't turn, but she felt it: Durgan's shadow flattening like a hound belly-crawling before a pounce.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Crossbows down,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin said, keeping his voice level.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "And we'll talk."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: One of the men smiled. It was not a good smile.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Talking's extra."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex moved. Not the dramatic leap of a stage knife but a hunter's step -- heel-to-ball, weight forward.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: A flick of wrist and the spearman's weapon wasn't in his hand anymore; it was in the ditch with a splash, and he was bleeding from the web of skin between thumb and forefinger in a way that made him as angry as it did clumsy.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Both crossbows thunked.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin didn't dodge.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Heat spiked up the lattice in his arm -- fast, focused, controlled. He punched his palm at the ground between them, not at the men. Air went hard in a fan and the ditchwater hissed -- stone to glass in a strip three feet wide.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The bolts hit the newborn slick and skated sideways into the hedge, thunk-thunk, harmless.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The pain arrived half a beat later. Not rupturing. Not yet. A white wire drawn through the filaments, singing in his teeth.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Last chance,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin said, breath thin.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Go home."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They didn't. The second crossbowman yelped something about

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "hazard pay"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: and crabbed sideways to get a shot. Durgan's blade touched his throat from behind before he finished the motion.

segments.speaker: durgan
segments.text: "Drop it,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Durgan said. Calm. Too calm.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The man dropped it. The bowstring twanged and sang a sad little note as it hit the glass strip and spun.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The spear carrier dove for his weapon in the ditch, came up with cold mud and a broken glove. Vex met him halfway -- rib to elbow, knee to thigh, heel to instep -- a conversation he lost quickly and with regret.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The last one bolted.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex let him run.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Tell your employer,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: she called,

segments.speaker: durgan
segments.text: "to upgrade the talent."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: She retrieved the dropped spear and snapped it over her knee just because it felt good. The rib reminded her immediately that it had opinions about that. She absorbed the flash of pain without showing it, mostly.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Durgan released the man he'd collared -- gently, considering -- and stepped back like he'd touched a stove. His nose was bleeding. He wiped it onto black cloth that wouldn't show it.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin exhaled, shakier than he wanted to be. The filaments along his forearm thrummed angrily then settled to their baseline ache -- manageable. For now.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "What was that with the ground?"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex asked.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Glass,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin said, flexing his hand.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Easier to slide on than stand on."

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Going to assume you can only do that a handful of times before you chew your arm off."

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Something like that."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: She nodded, cataloguing his limits the way she catalogued everything.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Noted."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They crossed the ditch at a dry spot Vex found upstream and cut through a copse of alder that smelled of cat-pee and river iron. The day leaned toward afternoon; clouds stacked to the west in slate wedges.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They found an abandoned charcoal clamp on the lee side of a low hill and took ten minutes to drink and breathe. Caelin's arm cooled to a sulk. Durgan watched his own shadow not move. Vex used a shard of Caelin's glass to trim a hanging thread from her sleeve with a surgeon's care.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Aldric set down his bag and looked at Vex.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Rib."

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "It's fine."

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "You winced when you broke that spear. Sit down."

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "I said -- "

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "I know what you said."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He was already opening the bag.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Sit down."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: She sat. He ran two fingers along the rib line with clinical detachment, found the break by her sharp intake of breath, and pronounced it the same as before -- cracked, not displaced, nothing to do but strap it and stop doing stupid things with it.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: She didn't comment on the last part. He didn't expect her to.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He checked Caelin's arm wrap while he was at it -- didn't touch the scale, just the bandaging.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Holding,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: he said, in the tone of a man noting a weather gauge. Then he packed his bag and stood.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Forest ahead?"

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Ironwood fringe by nightfall,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex said.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "If we keep moving."

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Stick to the hedge lines,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin said.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Off the road the rest of the day."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They rose and moved on -- quicker now, quieter. The hedgerows thinned to fern and dogrose. Crows followed at a polite distance.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They reached the Ironwood fringe at dusk, where the trees grew straight and dark and the bark smelled faintly of cold iron even when you knew it couldn't. A forgotten road -- older than any of them had a right to name -- cut through in a shallow camber, the stones slick with moss and time.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: At a crossroads of ruts and intention, a waystone stood hip-high -- granite freckled with lichen, nine faint sigils carved around its crown. Most folks would see old scratches. Caelin's scale saw a circuit.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The Ember warmed. Not hot. Not warning. Recognition.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex crouched and brushed dirt from the base, revealing a dwarf-stamp so worn it was almost a rumor.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Thornik would've loved this,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: she said, softer than she meant to.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Yeah,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin said.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He dug into his pocket and found Serana's ward-disc -- the little bronze coin stamped with a sunburst she'd pressed into his hand without a prayer. He set it on the waystone's flat top.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: For a heartbeat, the disc's lines flared dull gold, as if something in the stone remembered what warding felt like. A faint smell of struck flint lifted and was gone.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Still works,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex said.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "A little."

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Enough,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin said.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They ate cold trail-bread and harder cheese. Durgan drifted the perimeter like a boundary rider, never far, never comfortably close, the way a man walks when he doesn't trust the ground he's standing on.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: A moth the size of a thumb fluttered into the ward's slight radius and settled on the waystone. It fanned powdery wings, paused as if listening, then lifted into the dark. Somewhere far away, a fox barked twice.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin unrolled his bedroll beneath a blackthorn and only then checked his pack again. The cream note was gone. In its place, a strip of vellum narrower than a finger, rolled tight and tied with a thread that wasn't a color he could name.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He didn't open it.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The ribbon at his wrist lay quiet and cool. He resisted the itch to touch it.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Later, when the forest had stilled to the sounds ironwoods make -- sap whisper and the tiny crackle of frost forming -- he woke to a light on the northern horizon that wasn't the moon.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: A curtain of green-white shimmered and folded on itself like silk lit from within, then tore and sealed without sound. The hairs on his forearms rose; the scale's lattice hummed to a pitch he could feel in his teeth.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Wake,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: he said, already on one knee.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex sat up instantly, dagger half-drawn. Durgan turned his face north and didn't blink.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "What is that?"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex whispered.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Depthspire,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin said, certain without knowing how.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Something opened. Or closed."

segments.speaker: durgan
segments.text: "Doors,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Durgan said.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "You can hear them even when you can't."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: From the far side of the waystone, Aldric sat up from his bedroll and looked north. He said nothing for a long moment. The aurora folded and refolded, silent and wrong.

segments.speaker: durgan
segments.text: "I've seen that light once before,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: he said finally.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Two years ago, three weeks before Veln's Post."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: His voice was even -- not steady the way a calm man is steady, but steady the way a man is steady who has decided what he thinks about something and is done renegotiating.

segments.speaker: durgan
segments.text: "It wasn't a warning. It was a door opening. And thirty-seven men died to whatever came through."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: No one answered that. It didn't need an answer.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The light faded by degrees until the horizon was only horizon again, and the ironwoods went on pretending to be ordinary trees.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Two days' grace,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin said under his breath, thinking of prismatic dust and steady hands. He didn't explain it.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex didn't ask.

segments.speaker: durgan
segments.text: "Forest track or Merchant Road?"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: she asked instead.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Forest,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin said.

segments.speaker: durgan
segments.text: "Humans on the road. Scouts hate ironwood."

segments.speaker: durgan
segments.text: "Scouts hate wards,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Durgan added, nodding at the disc still warm with a memory of light.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Then forest,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex decided.

segments.speaker: durgan
segments.text: "We thread the old way."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They settled again to the kind of rest you take in pieces. Vex lay on her good shoulder and counted breaths until numbers didn't matter. Durgan watched his shadow not move and let the dark drink what it needed. Caelin held the unopened vellum and didn't open it.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Aldric sat with his back against the waystone, eyes open, doing the particular mathematics of a man who'd been told a door was opening and was calculating what might come through it.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: After a while he lay down, not because he thought he'd sleep, but because horizontal was better than the alternative.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The ironwoods creaked softly, like doors deciding when to be doors.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: At some point, far to the north, something knocked once. Not loud. Not near.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: A patient sound.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Counting.
